heroPoems.title: Leviathan
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Can you draw Leviathan out with a hook,
or press down his tongue with a cord?
Will he beg you for mercy,
or speak gentle words to you?
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Fuel the Wheel
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Life feeds life.
Give back,
or the wheel of order cracks.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: It was not Death, for I stood up
heroPoems.firstStanza:
It was not Death, for I stood up,
And all the Dead, lie down—
It was not Night, for all the Bells
Put out their Tongues, for Noon.
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: On Reason and Passion
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: I rehearsed my death until it felt like craft.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I rehearsed my death until it felt like craft.
I learned posture, silence, and exit.

heroPoems.title: Gerontion
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Thou hast nor youth nor age
But as it were an after dinner sleep
Dreaming of both.

heroPoems.title: I ask for a moment’s indulgence to sit
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I ask for a moment’s indulgence to sit by thy side. The works that I
have in hand I will finish afterwards.
Away from the sight of thy face my heart knows no rest nor respite, and
my work becomes an endless toil in a shoreless sea of toil.
Today the summer has come at my window with its sighs and murmurs; and
the bees are plying their minstrelsy at the court of the flowering
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Sonnets to Orpheus I, 5
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Set up no monument.
Let the rose bloom for him each year.
For this is Orpheus.
His metamorphosis in one thing and another.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Where Spring, the lord of the seasons, reigneth, there the
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Where Spring, the lord of the seasons, reigneth, there the
Unstruck Music sounds of itself,
There the streams of light flow in all directions;
Few are the men who can cross to that shore!
There, where millions of Krishnas stand with hands folded,
Where millions of Vishnus bow their heads,

collections.name: The Far End of Tenderness
collections.tagline: For when the kettle starts again.
collections.theme: Poems for the slow pivot from day-noise toward interior quiet.

featuredPoem.title: Count each passing hour as certain profit
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Count each passing hour as certain profit
what leaves the hand of fate does not return

poems.title: When I have fears that I may cease to be
Have you not heard his silent steps? He

collections.tagline: For rooms full of held breaths.
collections.theme: A thread of attention to rain, glass, paper, and hands at rest.

featuredPoem.title: My Witness in Heaven
featuredPoem.firstLines:
My spirit is broken,
my days are extinct,

poems.title: The Law of Action and Offering
Hang up the swing of love to-day! Hang the body and the mind

collections.name: Where Morning Arrives Slowly
collections.theme: Poems that make waiting feel textured rather than empty.

featuredPoem.title: Anecdote of the Jar
featuredPoem.firstLines:
I placed a jar in Tennessee,
And round it was, upon a hill.

poems.title: Because I could not stop for Death
